title: chylismia claviformis subsp. integrior
intro: Chylismia claviformis subsp. integrior, also known as the desert catchfly, is a perennial plant species native to the deserts of the southwestern United States. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce pain and inflammation. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Desert catchfly can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in topical preparations such as salves or ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
